headline: Meta is adding ridiculous 'rate limits' and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
summary: Meta introduces a $20/month subscription for its AI smart glasses. The 'Conversation Focus' feature will be limited to three hours monthly. Users must subscribe to Meta One Premium to bypass these new limits.

headline: Anthropic's long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is finally cleared to come back online. Access will be restored globally on Claude platforms starting Wednesday. This follows weeks of negotiations with the Trump administration.

headline: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
summary: Dish, the parent company of Dish TV and Sling TV, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. The filing allows the company to wind down its wireless operations. Dish TV and Sling TV services will continue to operate as usual.

headline: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
summary: Amazon was fined $2.25 million by the Federal Trade Commission. The fine addresses claims the company failed to aid identity theft victims. Amazon reportedly refused to provide information on fraudulent purchases.

headline: X-Men '97 Season 2, Episodes 1-3 Review & Recap (Full Spoilers)
summary: X-Men '97 Season 2 begins with a very strong start across its first three episodes. The series immediately explores the dramatic, Apocalypse-heavy aftermath of Season 1. Viewers dive straight into the fallout from previous events.

headline: Reacher Season 4 Returns in August, Neagley Spin-Off Lands the Following Month
summary: Reacher Season 4 is slated to premiere in August. A spin-off series focusing on Neagley will follow in September. Premiere dates are now confirmed for the popular Prime Video shows.

headline: The New Rick & Morty GLD Jewelry Drop Is Here and the Pickle Rick Pendant Already Sold Out
summary: A new collection of Rick & Morty GLD Jewelry has been released. The highly anticipated Pickle Rick pendant sold out almost immediately. Fans are rushing to get their hands on the latest themed merchandise.

headline: Ex-PlayStation Boss Defends PC Strategy as Sony Pivots Away from Multiplatform Releases
summary: Shawn Layden, former PlayStation CEO, defends past PC strategy. He explains why Sony's move into PC gaming made strategic sense. This comes as Sony reportedly shifts away from multiplatform releases.

headline: Woman raped by Superdry co-founder tells BBC she was working for him at the time
summary: A woman has told the BBC she was raped by Superdry co-founder James Holder. She alleges the attack occurred while she was working for him. She described feeling fear returning to her job after the incident.

headline: Chris Mason: Starmer's defence plan leaves crunching trade-offs for Burnham to confront
summary: BBC political editor Chris Mason analyzes Starmer's new defence plan. The plan presents difficult trade-offs for Andy Burnham. Mason reflects on the significant implications of the Defence Investment Plan.

headline: Plea for households to read energy meter as prices rise
summary: Households are urged to submit their energy meter readings promptly. Energy prices have increased by 13% annually for consumers. This surge follows Ofgem's latest price cap taking effect.

headline: Why Gen Z are planning for life without a state pension
summary: Many members of Generation Z are planning for retirement without a state pension. Younger people express widespread doubt that the state pension will exist when they are older. This reflects concerns about long-term financial stability.
